Compromise is a pathfinding problem.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLattice9 models enterprise networks not as a flat list of assets, but as a high-dimensional, bitemporally evolving directed multigraph. It executes \u003cstrong\u003e23 computational intelligence algorithms\u003c/strong\u003e across the topology — spanning graph field theory, topological data analysis (TDA), adversarial game theory, attack economics, wave propagation, causal inference, entropy collapse, and counterfactual simulation — to mathematically prioritize lateral path exposure and minimize analyst cognitive load.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIt does not scan. It does not dashboard. Instead of treating vulnerability flags as binary absolute truths, Lattice9 runs \u003cstrong\u003eDamped loopy Belief Propagation\u003c/strong\u003e sweeps to calculate a convergence confidence array \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$C_i$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-display-math\" style=\"display: block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$$m_{i \\to j}^{(t)} = (1 - \\alpha) \\cdot \\left( \\psi_{ij} \\cdot C_0(i) \\prod_{k \\in N(i) \\setminus {j}} m_{k \\to i}^{(t-1)} \\right) + \\alpha \\cdot m_{i \\to j}^{(t-1)}$$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo prevent cyclical positive-feedback loops from inflating confidence values on uncompromised nodes, our sweeps enforce a damping threshold \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$\\alpha$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e calibrated against the largest eigenvalue of the graph Laplacian \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$L$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-display-math\" style=\"display: block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$$\\alpha \u0026gt; 1 - \\frac{1}{\\rho_{\\text{max}}(L)}$$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e2. Constraint-Aware Dijkstra Traversals\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-2-constraint-aware-dijkstra-traversals\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 2. Constraint-Aware Dijkstra Traversals\" href=\"#2-constraint-aware-dijkstra-traversals\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAttack paths are not simple shortest-paths. Lattice9 filters paths deterministically by evaluating physical preconditions (operating systems, ingress port states, credential access privileges) during edge relaxation. If a prerequisite gate is closed, the edge cost evaluates to \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$\\infty$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e, routing path synthesis strictly around impossible vectors.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e3. Probabilistic Attack Economics\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-3-probabilistic-attack-economics\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 3. Probabilistic Attack Economics\" href=\"#3-probabilistic-attack-economics\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe planning traverser prioritizes pathways by maximizing \u003cstrong\u003eAttacker ROI/Utility\u003c/strong\u003e \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$\\mathcal{U}(P)$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-display-math\" style=\"display: block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$$\\mathcal{U}(P) = \\frac{\\sum_{v \\in P} \\text{Gain}(v) \\cdot \\prod_{e \\in P} P_{\\text{traverse}}(e)}{\\text{Risk}_{\\text{detection}}(P) \\cdot \\sum_{e \\in P} \\text{Cost}_{\\text{operational}}(e)}$$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePaths are dynamically ranked, highlighting stealth-optimal routes (minimizing detection visibility) vs speed-optimal routes (minimizing execution complexity).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e4. Graph Field Theory\" href=\"#4-graph-field-theory\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLattice9 models threat attraction wells by calculating the \u003cstrong\u003eAttack Pressure Field\u003c/strong\u003e \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$\\Phi(v)$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e generated by high-value, highly vulnerable assets:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-display-math\" style=\"display: block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$$\\Phi(v) = \\sum_{u \\in V \\setminus {v}} \\frac{\\text{Risk}(u) \\cdot \\text{Trust}(u, v)}{d(u, v)^\\gamma}$$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhere \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$d(u,v)$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e is the geodesic distance on the topology, and \u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-inline-math\" style=\"display: inline-block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$\\gamma$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e is our field spatial decay factor. This highlights natural gravity wells where credential compromise propagates fastest.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e5. Lattice9 applies a temporal half-life decay function to edge weights and finding confidences:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cmath-renderer class=\"js-display-math\" style=\"display: block\" data-run-id=\"59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd\"\u003e$$C_t(e) = C_0(e) \\cdot e^{-\\lambda (t - t_{\\text{update}})}$$\u003c/math-renderer\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis automatically decays the validity of aged or inactive credentials, simulating natural environmental drift.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e6. Stream Broker\nREDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0\n\n# Sovereign Engine Key\nLATTICE9_ENGINE_KEY=sovereign-l9-secret-2026\n\n# Fallback LLM API keys (if Proxima is bypassed)\nOPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-...\nANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Neo4j Graph Database\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eNEO4J_URI\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ebolt://localhost:7687\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eNEO4J_USER\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eneo4j\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eNEO4J_PASSWORD\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eyour_secure_password\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e PostgreSQL Transactional Database\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eDATABASE_URL\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003epostgresql://postgres:postgres_password@localhost:5432/lattice9\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Redis Queue \u0026amp; Stream Broker\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eREDIS_URL\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eredis://localhost:6379/0\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Sovereign Engine Key\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eLATTICE9_ENGINE_KEY\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003esovereign-l9-secret-2026\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Fallback LLM API keys (if Proxima is bypassed)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eOPENAI_API_KEY\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003esk-proj-...\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eANTHROPIC_API_KEY\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003esk-ant-api03-...\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e3. Each module mathematically changes how paths are traversed, how confidence propagates, or how exposure is prioritized. Every equation directly guides lateral movement reasoning.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e2. The \u003cstrong\u003eGraph is the Sovereign Source of Truth\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen an agent makes an assertion, the statement must be anchored to a specific Neo4j node or relationship. If the telemetry state changes, the drift engine triggers active recomputation. <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The Lattice9 Mission</h2><a id="user-content-the-lattice9-mission" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The Lattice9 Mission" href="#the-lattice9-mission"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-important" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-report mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 1.75C0 .784.784 0 1.75 0h12.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v9.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 13H8.06l-2.573 2.573A1.458 1.458 0 0 1 3 14.543V13H1.75A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 11.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v9.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h2a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.19l2.72-2.72a.749.749 0 0 1 .53-.22h6.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-9.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Zm7 2.25v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 9a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Important</p><p dir="auto"><strong>Infrastructure is a graph. Vulnerabilities are edges. Compromise is a pathfinding problem.</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">Lattice9 models enterprise networks not as a flat list of assets, but as a high-dimensional, bitemporally evolving directed multigraph. It executes <strong>23 computational intelligence algorithms</strong> across the topology — spanning graph field theory, topological data analysis (TDA), adversarial game theory, attack economics, wave propagation, causal inference, entropy collapse, and counterfactual simulation — to mathematically prioritize lateral path exposure and minimize analyst cognitive load.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>It does not scan. It does not dashboard. It computes.</strong></p>
</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Architectural Comparison</h2><a id="user-content-architectural-comparison" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Architectural Comparison" href="#architectural-comparison"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Traditional offensive-security platforms optimize for scan execution and vulnerability listing. Lattice9 operates on stateful adversarial graph cognition.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th align="left">Dimension</th>
<th align="left">Legacy Security Tools</th>
<th align="left">Lattice9 Cognition Engine</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Core Data Model</strong></td>
<td align="left">Flat, disconnected table of findings</td>
<td align="left">Typed, directed, high-dimensional multigraph</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Path Prioritization</strong></td>
<td align="left">Static CVSS severity scoring</td>
<td align="left">Attacker ROI utility optimized over topological context</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Temporal State</strong></td>
<td align="left">Per-engagement scan snapshots</td>
<td align="left">Persistent, bitemporally evolving graph memory</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Evidence Validation</strong></td>
<td align="left">Static screenshots and log attachments</td>
<td align="left">Cryptographically provable evidence lineage DAGs</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Confidence Metric</strong></td>
<td align="left">Binary vulnerability flags</td>
<td align="left">Bayesian belief propagation under uncertainty</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Exploit Chains</strong></td>
<td align="left">Manual analyst correlation</td>
<td align="left">Constraint-aware Dijkstra & manifold geodesic routing</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left"><strong>Reasoning Model</strong></td>
<td align="left">Ad-hoc analyst intuition</td>
<td align="left">Multi-agent debate grounded strictly in graph states</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Core System Architecture</h2><a id="user-content-core-system-architecture" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Core System Architecture" href="#core-system-architecture"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">High-Dimensional Graph Model</h2><a id="user-content-high-dimensional-graph-model" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: High-Dimensional Graph Model" href="#high-dimensional-graph-model"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Lattice9 formalizes target infrastructure mathematically as a bitemporally evolving directed multigraph <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi>G</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer>:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <msub>
    <mi>G</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo>=</mo>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <msub>
    <mi>V</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo>,</mo>
  <msub>
    <mi>E</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo>,</mo>
  <msub>
    <mi>W</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo>,</mo>
  <msub>
    <mi mathvariant="normal">Φ</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<p dir="auto">Where:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi>V</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer> is the set of heterogeneous network entities (Hosts, Services, Credentials, Identities, Vulnerabilities, Evidence).</li>
<li>
<math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi>E</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer> is the set of typed, directed relationships mapping connectivity and trust privileges (<code>TRUSTS</code>, <code>AUTHENTICATES_TO</code>, <code>HOSTS</code>, <code>PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION</code>).</li>
<li>
<math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi>W</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer> is the multi-dimensional edge weight matrix defining transition cost, monitoring friction, and target resistance.</li>
<li>
<math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi mathvariant="normal">Φ</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer> is the dynamic graph field state governing compromise energy propagation.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">23 Computational Intelligence Modules</h2><a id="user-content-23-computational-intelligence-modules" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 23 Computational Intelligence Modules" href="#23-computational-intelligence-modules"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Every mathematical model in Lattice9 maps to an operational consequence in lateral path computation. There is no decorative mathematics.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. Bayesian Belief Propagation</h3><a id="user-content-1-bayesian-belief-propagation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. Bayesian Belief Propagation" href="#1-bayesian-belief-propagation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Enterprise telemetry is noisy, conflicting, and dynamic. Instead of treating vulnerability flags as binary absolute truths, Lattice9 runs <strong>Damped loopy Belief Propagation</strong> sweeps to calculate a convergence confidence array <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msub>
    <mi>C</mi>
    <mi>i</mi>
  </msub>
</math></math-renderer>:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <msubsup>
    <mi>m</mi>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mi>i</mi>
      <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
      <mi>j</mi>
    </mrow>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>t</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
  </msubsup>
  <mo>=</mo>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mn>1</mn>
  <mo>−</mo>
  <mi>α</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
  <mo>⋅</mo>
  <mrow data-mjx-texclass="INNER">
    <mo data-mjx-texclass="OPEN">(</mo>
    <msub>
      <mi>ψ</mi>
      <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
        <mi>i</mi>
        <mi>j</mi>
      </mrow>
    </msub>
    <mo>⋅</mo>
    <msub>
      <mi>C</mi>
      <mn>0</mn>
    </msub>
    <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
    <mi>i</mi>
    <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    <munder>
      <mo data-mjx-texclass="OP">∏</mo>
      <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
        <mi>k</mi>
        <mo>∈</mo>
        <mi>N</mi>
        <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
        <mi>i</mi>
        <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
        <mo>∖</mo>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mi>j</mi>
        </mrow>
      </mrow>
    </munder>
    <msubsup>
      <mi>m</mi>
      <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
        <mi>k</mi>
        <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
        <mi>i</mi>
      </mrow>
      <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
        <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
        <mi>t</mi>
        <mo>−</mo>
        <mn>1</mn>
        <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
      </mrow>
    </msubsup>
    <mo data-mjx-texclass="CLOSE">)</mo>
  </mrow>
  <mo>+</mo>
  <mi>α</mi>
  <mo>⋅</mo>
  <msubsup>
    <mi>m</mi>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mi>i</mi>
      <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
      <mi>j</mi>
    </mrow>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>t</mi>
      <mo>−</mo>
      <mn>1</mn>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
  </msubsup>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<p dir="auto">To prevent cyclical positive-feedback loops from inflating confidence values on uncompromised nodes, our sweeps enforce a damping threshold <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi>α</mi>
</math></math-renderer> calibrated against the largest eigenvalue of the graph Laplacian <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi>L</mi>
</math></math-renderer>:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <mi>α</mi>
  <mo>></mo>
  <mn>1</mn>
  <mo>−</mo>
  <mfrac>
    <mn>1</mn>
    <mrow>
      <msub>
        <mi>ρ</mi>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mtext>max</mtext>
        </mrow>
      </msub>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>L</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
  </mfrac>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. Constraint-Aware Dijkstra Traversals</h3><a id="user-content-2-constraint-aware-dijkstra-traversals" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. Constraint-Aware Dijkstra Traversals" href="#2-constraint-aware-dijkstra-traversals"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Attack paths are not simple shortest-paths. Lattice9 filters paths deterministically by evaluating physical preconditions (operating systems, ingress port states, credential access privileges) during edge relaxation. If a prerequisite gate is closed, the edge cost evaluates to <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi mathvariant="normal">∞</mi>
</math></math-renderer>, routing path synthesis strictly around impossible vectors.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">3. Probabilistic Attack Economics</h3><a id="user-content-3-probabilistic-attack-economics" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3. Probabilistic Attack Economics" href="#3-probabilistic-attack-economics"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The planning traverser prioritizes pathways by maximizing <strong>Attacker ROI/Utility</strong> <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
    <mi data-mjx-variant="-tex-calligraphic" mathvariant="script">U</mi>
  </mrow>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>P</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
</math></math-renderer>:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
    <mi data-mjx-variant="-tex-calligraphic" mathvariant="script">U</mi>
  </mrow>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>P</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
  <mo>=</mo>
  <mfrac>
    <mrow>
      <munder>
        <mo data-mjx-texclass="OP">∑</mo>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mi>v</mi>
          <mo>∈</mo>
          <mi>P</mi>
        </mrow>
      </munder>
      <mtext>Gain</mtext>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>v</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
      <mo>⋅</mo>
      <munder>
        <mo data-mjx-texclass="OP">∏</mo>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mi>e</mi>
          <mo>∈</mo>
          <mi>P</mi>
        </mrow>
      </munder>
      <msub>
        <mi>P</mi>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mtext>traverse</mtext>
        </mrow>
      </msub>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>e</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
    <mrow>
      <msub>
        <mtext>Risk</mtext>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mtext>detection</mtext>
        </mrow>
      </msub>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>P</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
      <mo>⋅</mo>
      <munder>
        <mo data-mjx-texclass="OP">∑</mo>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mi>e</mi>
          <mo>∈</mo>
          <mi>P</mi>
        </mrow>
      </munder>
      <msub>
        <mtext>Cost</mtext>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mtext>operational</mtext>
        </mrow>
      </msub>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>e</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
  </mfrac>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<p dir="auto">Paths are dynamically ranked, highlighting stealth-optimal routes (minimizing detection visibility) vs speed-optimal routes (minimizing execution complexity).</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">4. Graph Field Theory</h3><a id="user-content-4-graph-field-theory" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 4. Graph Field Theory" href="#4-graph-field-theory"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Lattice9 models threat attraction wells by calculating the <strong>Attack Pressure Field</strong> <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi mathvariant="normal">Φ</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>v</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
</math></math-renderer> generated by high-value, highly vulnerable assets:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <mi mathvariant="normal">Φ</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>v</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
  <mo>=</mo>
  <munder>
    <mo data-mjx-texclass="OP">∑</mo>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mi>u</mi>
      <mo>∈</mo>
      <mi>V</mi>
      <mo>∖</mo>
      <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
        <mi>v</mi>
      </mrow>
    </mrow>
  </munder>
  <mfrac>
    <mrow>
      <mtext>Risk</mtext>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>u</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
      <mo>⋅</mo>
      <mtext>Trust</mtext>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>u</mi>
      <mo>,</mo>
      <mi>v</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
    <mrow>
      <mi>d</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>u</mi>
      <mo>,</mo>
      <mi>v</mi>
      <msup>
        <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
        <mi>γ</mi>
      </msup>
    </mrow>
  </mfrac>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<p dir="auto">Where <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi>d</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>u</mi>
  <mo>,</mo>
  <mi>v</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
</math></math-renderer> is the geodesic distance on the topology, and <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mi>γ</mi>
</math></math-renderer> is our field spatial decay factor. This highlights natural gravity wells where credential compromise propagates fastest.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5. Temporal Cognition & Exponential Decay</h3><a id="user-content-5-temporal-cognition--exponential-decay" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5. Temporal Cognition & Exponential Decay" href="#5-temporal-cognition--exponential-decay"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Trust structures and credentials decay in validity over time. Lattice9 applies a temporal half-life decay function to edge weights and finding confidences:</p>
<p dir="auto"><math-renderer class="js-display-math" style="display: block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block">
  <msub>
    <mi>C</mi>
    <mi>t</mi>
  </msub>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>e</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
  <mo>=</mo>
  <msub>
    <mi>C</mi>
    <mn>0</mn>
  </msub>
  <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
  <mi>e</mi>
  <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
  <mo>⋅</mo>
  <msup>
    <mi>e</mi>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mo>−</mo>
      <mi>λ</mi>
      <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
      <mi>t</mi>
      <mo>−</mo>
      <msub>
        <mi>t</mi>
        <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
          <mtext>update</mtext>
        </mrow>
      </msub>
      <mo stretchy="false">)</mo>
    </mrow>
  </msup>
</math></math-renderer></p>
<p dir="auto">This automatically decays the validity of aged or inactive credentials, simulating natural environmental drift.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">6. Summary Matrix of Intelligence Modules</h3><a id="user-content-6-summary-matrix-of-intelligence-modules" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 6. Summary Matrix of Intelligence Modules" href="#6-summary-matrix-of-intelligence-modules"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

</tr>

<td align="left"><strong>Graph Neural</strong></td>
<td align="left"><math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <msubsup>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">
      <mi mathvariant="bold">h</mi>
    </mrow>
    <mi>v</mi>
    <mrow data-mjx-texclass="ORD">

<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<strong>Sequential Mode</strong>: Standard linear feed-forward pipeline (Plan <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
</math></math-renderer> Recon <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
</math></math-renderer> Correlate <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
</math></math-renderer> Exploit <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
</math></math-renderer> Verify <math-renderer class="js-inline-math" style="display: inline-block" data-run-id="59dac4f26fbd61a5631baa7e2828e6fd" data-catalyst=""><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
  <mo accent="false" stretchy="false">→</mo>
</math></math-renderer> Report).</li>
<li>
<strong>Parallel Mode</strong>: Concurrent execution of sub-tasks with analytical results merged asynchronously.</li>
<li>
<strong>Debate Mode</strong>: Dialectic model sweeps where agents challenge finding validity over round-based iterations before committing to Neo4j.</li>
<li>
<strong>Round-Robin Mode</strong>: Cyclic sweeps matching execution patterns until graph confidence updates stabilize.</li>
</ul>

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>1. OBJECTIVE: Analyze exposure of target subnet `10.100.0.0/16`.
   │
2. PLANNER decomposes into task DAG:
   ├── Task 1: CIDR network discovery & port scan
   ├── Task 2: Fingerprint exposed web & AD services
   ├── Task 3: Map credential configurations & trust boundaries
   └── Task 4: Trace exploit path and compute Attacker ROI
   │
3. RECON executes scans via MCP:
   ├── Identified 14 active hosts (host_0 to host_13)
   ├── Discovered open ports: host_3 (Nginx 1.24), host_7 (MS-RPC)
   └── Logged CVE-2024-24989 vulnerability on host_3 Nginx service
   │
4. CORRELATION maps topological relations:
   ├── host_3 (Nginx) RESOLVES_TO 10.100.2.14
   ├── host_3 HOSTS user session for `domain_backup_user`
   └── `domain_backup_user` HAS_PRIVILEGE on Domain Controller (host_10)
   │
5. EXPLOIT synthesizes constraint exploit chains:
   ├── Path A: CVE-2024-24989 (host_3) -> RCE -> Dump `domain_backup_user` -> DC (host_10)
   └── Validated preconditions: Target Nginx version matches; target port 443 open.
   │
6. VERIFICATION skeptic audits findings:
   ├── CVE-2024-24989: Confirmed via banner matching (Confidence: 0.92)
   └── AD Trust path: Checked session active state (Confidence: 0.81)
   │
7. REPORT compiles systems summary:
   ├── Ranks Path A as highest utility due to minimal stealth cost
   └── Computes overall Attack Pressure Field highlighting host_3 as gravity well
   │
8. MEMORY commits snapshots to database:
   ├── Creates Neo4j versioned snapshot `snapshot_v9_04`
   └── Triggers Redis stream event `stream:graph:mutations` to update engine caches
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Deployment & Getting Started</h2><a id="user-content-deployment--getting-started" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Deployment & Getting Started" href="#deployment--getting-started"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">System Requirements</h3><a id="user-content-system-requirements" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: System Requirements" href="#system-requirements"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>OS</strong>: Linux (Ubuntu 22.04+ recommended) or macOS</li>
<li><strong>Graph Store</strong>: Neo4j 5.x (local or remote instance)</li>
<li><strong>Broker</strong>: Redis 7.x (required for stream processing and async tasks)</li>
<li><strong>Database</strong>: PostgreSQL 16+ (with <code>pgvector</code> extension)</li>
<li><strong>Runtime</strong>: Python 3.11+ and Node.js 20+</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. Repository Setup</h3><a id="user-content-1-repository-setup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. Repository Setup" href="#1-repository-setup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Clone the repository</span>
git clone https://github.com/webspoilt/lattice9.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> lattice9

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Instantiate virtual environment</span>
python -m venv .venv
<span class="pl-c1">source</span> .venv/bin/activate  <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install requirements</span>
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> server-py
pip install -r requirements.txt</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/webspoilt/lattice9.git
cd lattice9

# Instantiate virtual environment
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate

# Install requirements
cd server-py
pip install -r requirements.txt" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. Environment Configuration</h3><a id="user-content-2-environment-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. Environment Configuration" href="#2-environment-configuration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Create a <code>.env</code> file inside <code>server-py/</code> referencing your backend credentials:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-dotenv notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Neo4j Graph Database</span>
<span class="pl-v">NEO4J_URI</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">bolt://localhost:7687</span>
<span class="pl-v">NEO4J_USER</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">neo4j</span>
<span class="pl-v">NEO4J_PASSWORD</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">your_secure_password</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> PostgreSQL Transactional Database</span>
<span class="pl-v">DATABASE_URL</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">postgresql://postgres:postgres_password@localhost:5432/lattice9</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Redis Queue & Stream Broker</span>
<span class="pl-v">REDIS_URL</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">redis://localhost:6379/0</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Sovereign Engine Key</span>
<span class="pl-v">LATTICE9_ENGINE_KEY</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">sovereign-l9-secret-2026</span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Fallback LLM API keys (if Proxima is bypassed)</span>
<span class="pl-v">OPENAI_API_KEY</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">sk-proj-...</span>
<span class="pl-v">ANTHROPIC_API_KEY</span><span class="pl-k">=</span><span class="pl-s">sk-ant-api03-...</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Neo4j Graph Database
NEO4J_URI=bolt://localhost:7687
NEO4J_USER=neo4j
NEO4J_PASSWORD=your_secure_password

# PostgreSQL Transactional Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres_password@localhost:5432/lattice9

# Redis Queue & Stream Broker
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0

# Sovereign Engine Key
LATTICE9_ENGINE_KEY=sovereign-l9-secret-2026

# Fallback LLM API keys (if Proxima is bypassed)
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-..." tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Execution Philosophy</h2><a id="user-content-execution-philosophy" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Execution Philosophy" href="#execution-philosophy"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. Rigorous Science Over Tool Spam</h3><a id="user-content-1-rigorous-science-over-tool-spam" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. Rigorous Science Over Tool Spam" href="#1-rigorous-science-over-tool-spam"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Traditional vulnerability platforms measure success by the count of integrated wrapping tools—"over 500 scanners supported!"—producing massive alerts of un-exploitable noise.</p>
<p dir="auto">Lattice9 executes exactly <strong>23 rigorous mathematical modules</strong>. Each module mathematically changes how paths are traversed, how confidence propagates, or how exposure is prioritized. Every equation directly guides lateral movement reasoning.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. Observable Graph-State Grounding</h3><a id="user-content-2-observable-graph-state-grounding" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. Observable Graph-State Grounding" href="#2-observable-graph-state-grounding"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">We eliminate AI hallucination by architectural constraints rather than prompt engineering. The <strong>Graph is the Sovereign Source of Truth</strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto">When an agent makes an assertion, the statement must be anchored to a specific Neo4j node or relationship. If the telemetry state changes, the drift engine triggers active recomputation. No agent can hypothesize a vulnerability or attack vector that violates the physical target state.</p>
<hr>
